DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Patrina Harrison; 415-567-8182; Plaintiff in Pro per. (In-person appearance)
Terrance J. Evans,ESQ.; DUANE MORRIS LLP; 415 957 3000; tjevans@duanemorris.com; for Defendant JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. (Appearance via Zoom Conference)

Defendant, JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. contests the Courts' tentative ruling.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A's demurrer to plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is overruled as to causes of action 1 through 8. The Court liberally construes plaintiff's allegations pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 452. In addition, the Court accepts plaintiff's allegations as true when ruling on a demurrer. (See Curtis T. v. County of Los Angeles (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1405, 1412, fn. 3.) Plaintiff alleges that defendant's employee/agent acted with an intent to harm plaintiff when he reached for his service weapon, positioned himself to shoot plaintiff, and then disclosed that defendant has a policy to shoot African American customers. (Second Amended Complaint, pars. 2, 4, 6-7, 9.) The Second Amended Complaint alleges threatening conduct that supports causes of action 1 through 8. (See, e.g., People v. Chance (2008) 44 Cal.4th 1164, 1168 [an assault may be committed even if the defendant is several steps away from actually inflicting injury].) The Court agrees that conspiracy (cause of action 3) and respondeat superior (cause of action 6) are not standalone claims. The Court treats these allegations as general charging allegations that can support vicarious liability. The Court, however, notes that a corporation generally cannot conspire with itself and its employees. (See Black v. Bank of America (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 1, 5-6.)

The demurrer to cause of action 9 is sustained without leave to amend. (See Code Civ. Proc. Section 430.10(e).) "Harassing conduct" is not a cognizable claim under these circumstances. Plaintiff refers to inapposite employment and Code of Civil Procedure statutes.

Defendant's requests that the Court take judicial notice of other complaints and charges filed by plaintiff is denied.

Court notes that the proposed order emailed to the court by plaintiff did not track the Court's tentative ruling. The Court prepares and signs an order that is verbatim with the Courts ruling.

DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A's motion to strike is denied. The Court liberally construes plaintiff's allegations pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 452. In addition, the Court accepts plaintiff's allegations as true when ruling on a motion to strike. (See Willis v. City of Carlsbad (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 1104, 1121.) Plaintiff alleges that defendant's employee/agent acted with an intent to harm plaintiff when he reached for his service weapon, positioned himself to shoot plaintiff, and then disclosed that defendant has a policy to shoot African American customers. (Second Amended Complaint, pars. 2, 4, 6-7, 9.) Such allegations plead malicious conduct and a factual issue remains whether a corporate officer, director, or managing agent authorized, ratified, or had advance knowledge of the alleged misconduct.

PLAINTIFF PATRINA HARRISON's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint is granted.

Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 473 states a court may allow to amend any pleading or proceeding in furtherance of justice. The policy of great liberality in amendment of pleadings "is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial to leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1422, 1428.) While Defendant is correct that Plaintiff failed to comply with the technical requirements outlined in Rule of Court 3.1324 (a) and (b), it does not contend that it will be prejudiced by the amendment or that Plaintiff has delayed unduly. The parties are reminded that if defendant wishes to demur to the second amended complaint, it must first meet and confer with plaintiff pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41.
